description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metallic frame. The solar panel array is elevated above a base platform, which appears to house electrical components and possibly an inverter. The frame is constructed from metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing structural support and stability. The base includes several visible connectors, switches, and possibly cooling fins, indicating integrated power management hardware. The overall design is modular and industrial, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility sites. The color scheme is predominantly gray and black, with some blue and metallic accents.
description: A compact, rectangular solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metal base with visible electrical components and connectors, designed for urban energy generation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow edge of the solar panel array and the main access panel with connectors and switches.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the length of the solar panel array and the detailed structure of the base, including multiple connectors and hardware.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back edge of the solar panel and additional access points or cooling elements on the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the full width of the solar panel array and the arrangement of electrical components on the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a metallic frame, likely aluminum or steel
